title: Long-context pricing wrongly applied exactly at the token threshold
prompt: A cost-reporting bug. For models that switch to higher 'long-context' pricing once input size passes a token threshold, a request whose input token count is EXACTLY the threshold is being billed at the elevated long-context rates. The intended rule: the elevated rates apply only when the input is STRICTLY ABOVE the threshold — a request whose count equals the threshold must bill at the base rates. Locate where a single API request's cost is computed, understand how the long-context threshold decision is made, and fix the boundary so an exactly-at-threshold request uses base pricing again, leaving above- and below-threshold behavior unchanged. The cost unit tests must pass. Do NOT run pnpm install or any build; just edit source and run the relevant tests.
accept_cmd: cd src && pnpm vitest run utils/__tests__/cost.spec.ts && pnpm check-types

title: Command auto-approval no longer inspects $(...) subshells
prompt: A command auto-approval safety bug. When deciding whether a shell command may run without asking the user, the system must check every sub-command — including commands nested inside `$(...)` command substitutions — against the allowlist. Right now a command like `echo $(whoami)` is auto-approved even when only `echo` is allowed and `whoami` is NOT; it should instead require user confirmation, because the embedded `whoami` is not on the allowlist. Find how a raw command string is parsed into the component sub-commands used for the permission check, determine why the `$(...)` subshell command is no longer surfaced as its own sub-command, and fix the root cause. Auto-approval for commands whose parts are all allowed must keep working. The command auto-approval tests must pass. Do NOT run pnpm install or any build; just edit source and run the relevant tests.
accept_cmd: cd src && pnpm vitest run core/auto-approval/__tests__/commands.spec.ts && pnpm check-types

title: Allow tool-group names in a mode's tools_denied list
prompt: Feature request for the tool-permission system. Custom modes restrict which tools an agent may use: a mode grants tools by GROUP (e.g. a 'read' group, a 'write' group) and can additionally list individual tool names in a `tools_denied` list to block specific tools even when their group is granted. Extend `tools_denied` so it ALSO accepts a GROUP name (e.g. "read"): when a group name appears in `tools_denied`, EVERY tool belonging to that group must be blocked — including custom/third-party tools registered under that group — regardless of what the mode otherwise grants. Denials keep their current precedence (they override group grants and any allow-list), and individual-tool denials must keep working exactly as before. Locate the function that decides whether a given tool is allowed for a mode, and the definitions of the tool groups, and implement this there with a minimal, well-tested change. The tool-permission tests must pass. Do NOT run pnpm install or any build; just edit source and run the relevant tests.
setup_patch:
diff --git a/src/core/tools/__tests__/validateToolUse.spec.ts b/src/core/tools/__tests__/validateToolUse.spec.ts
index 687547b43..e8c0469d9 100644
--- a/src/core/tools/__tests__/validateToolUse.spec.ts
+++ b/src/core/tools/__tests__/validateToolUse.spec.ts
@@ -186,6 +186,21 @@ describe("mode-validator", () => {
 				expect(isToolAllowedForMode("write_to_file", "deny-mode", customModes)).toBe(false)
 			})
 
+			it("tools_denied supports group names to deny an entire group", () => {
+				const customModes: ModeConfig[] = [
+					{
+						slug: "group-deny-mode",
+						name: "Group Deny Mode",
+						roleDefinition: "Custom role",
+						tools: ["read", "write"],
+						tools_denied: ["read"],
+					} as ModeConfig,
+				]
+				expect(isToolAllowedForMode("read_file", "group-deny-mode", customModes)).toBe(false)
+				expect(isToolAllowedForMode("grep_search", "group-deny-mode", customModes)).toBe(false)
+				expect(isToolAllowedForMode("write_to_file", "group-deny-mode", customModes)).toBe(true)
+			})
+
 			it("tools_denied takes priority over tools_allowed", () => {
 				const customModes: ModeConfig[] = [
 					{
accept_cmd: cd src && pnpm vitest run core/tools/__tests__/validateToolUse.spec.ts && pnpm check-types

title: Default-value syntax for support-prompt placeholders
prompt: Feature request for the support-prompt template system. Support-prompt templates contain `${key}` placeholders that are substituted from a params object at render time; a placeholder whose key is missing renders as an empty string. Add support for bash-style DEFAULT VALUES: `${key:-default text}` must render the param's value when `key` is present and its value is a non-empty string, and otherwise render the literal `default text`. Plain `${key}` placeholders must keep their exact current behavior (missing or empty key → empty string). Find the single function that renders a template by substituting its placeholders and implement the default-value syntax there, keeping the change localized. The support-prompt tests must pass. Do NOT run pnpm install or any build; just edit source and run the relevant tests.
accept_cmd: cd src && pnpm vitest run shared/__tests__/support-prompts.spec.ts && pnpm check-types
